title: Calm Is My Natural Default
subtitle: An affirmation for anchoring calm as a trained, reachable state rather than a lucky one
excerpt: A brief affirmation for the middle of a busy or stressful day. The statement names calm as something that is being built through practice — not a personality trait you either have or don't, but a state the nervous system returns to more easily with each repetition.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 25 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use mid-day when stress has been running for several hours and you want to interrupt the pattern before it builds into the evening.

content.text: A short affirmation for the middle of a day that has been running at speed. The statement names calm as a default that is being trained through practice — not a fixed trait or a lucky coincidence, but something the body is learning to find more quickly each time it's reached for.
Use it at a transition: between tasks, after a tense call, before switching from one context to another. One breath first, then the statement.
Calm is my natural default. I return to it more easily each day. My body knows this state and trusts it.
Say it three times quietly. The 'my body knows this state' line does the work — it names calm as already familiar, already established, rather than something you are reaching toward from a distance.
Adapted from the Day 25 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
